pattern: Binary search over the answer space finds the integer square root by halving the candidate range each step instead of testing every value.
hints: You want the largest integer whose square does not exceed x, so the answer lives somewhere in a sorted range you can probe.
Checking a candidate c is cheap: c*c <= x tells you whether c is feasible, and feasibility is monotone, so binary search applies.
Search the range 0..x; for the midpoint m, if m*m <= x keep m as a possible answer and move the lower bound up, otherwise move the upper bound down, returning the best feasible m.

edge_inputs.description: non-perfect square truncates down, not rounds
perfect square of a larger value
two and three both truncate to one

stress.description: values across the whole range, including some perfect squares
stress.note: perfect-square branch ensures exact boundaries are hit
